content.content:
1→## Healing Knowledge Base (KB): Consistent Learning & Deduplication
     2→
     3→Purpose: Persist cross-run learning for self-healing while jobs remain ephemeral. Avoid duplication via content-addressed keys and canonicalization.
     4→
     5→### Store
     6→- SeaweedFS (artifacts) + Consul KV (locks/coordination)
     7→- Layout:
     8→  - `kb/healing/errors/{lang}/{signature}/cases/{run_id}.json` — append-only cases
     9→  - `kb/healing/errors/{lang}/{signature}/summary.json` — promoted fixes + stats (win-rate, recency, size)
    10→  - `kb/healing/patches/{patch_fingerprint}.patch` — normalized diffs
    11→  - `kb/healing/index/{date}/vectors.*` — optional vector index bundles
    12→
    13→### Canonicalization
    14→- Error signature: normalize stdout/stderr (strip paths/timestamps), hash: `sha256(lang|compiler|normalized_error[:N])`
    15→- Patch fingerprint: normalize unified diff (strip timestamps/whitespace), hash content
    16→- Context hash: language, lane, top-level deps (pom/go.mod/gradle) checksums
    17→
    18→### Read Path (Planner)
    19→1) Load `summary.json` by signature; suggest top-ranked fixes (ORW recipe or patch fingerprint)
    20→2) If no direct match, optionally load nearest neighbors via vector index bundles
    21→3) Fall back to LLM-generated plan
    22→
    23→### Write Path (Post-branch)
    24→- Write full `cases/{run_id}.json` with inputs, outputs, outcomes, versions
    25→- Store new `patches/{fingerprint}.patch` once (dedup)
    26→- Optimistically update `summary.json` under Consul KV CAS/lock; otherwise compactor will recalc later
    27→
    28→### Promotion & Ranking
    29→- Promote a fix after ≥2 successes across distinct repos/context hashes
    30→- Score = win-rate + small bonus for smaller/faster patches + recency; demote unstable patches
    31→
    32→### Compactor Job (Periodic)
    33→- Scans cases, recomputes summaries and vector bundles
    34→- Writes manifest `kb/healing/snapshot.json` (timestamp, counts, versions)
    35→- Prunes stale/blacklisted items
    36→
    37→### Governance & Safety
    38→- Blacklist: `kb/healing/blacklist/{signature}.json` or `{patch_fingerprint}.json`
    39→- All edits go through diff-apply pipeline; tools default-deny; network allowlists explicit
    40→
    41→### Locks & CAS
    42→- Summary update lock key: `kb/locks/{lang}/{signature}` with a short TTL (e.g., 5s) and CAS token.
    43→- Planner/branches: if lock acquisition fails, write case only; compactor will rebuild summary.
    44→- Compactor: uses lock keys to serialize per-signature summary writes; writes to temp then atomic rename.
    45→
    46→### Sanitization
    47→- Before persisting `cases/` records, sanitize `stdout/stderr` and any user content:
    48→  - Mask tokens (e.g., `ghp_`, `gitlab_pat`, JWT patterns) and credentials in URLs.
    49→  - Strip absolute paths and secrets-like envs.
    50→  - Truncate excessively long logs.
    51→

1→## MVP: transflow (Minimal Viable Implementation)
     2→
     3→Purpose: deliver a basic, working transflow that can apply OpenRewrite recipes, heal build errors via LangGraph planner/reducer jobs with parallel options, and open a GitLab MR.
     4→
     5→## Implementation Status
     6→
     7→✅ **Fully Implemented:**
     8→- OpenRewrite recipe execution with ARF integration
     9→- Build check via `/v1/apps/:app/builds` (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    10→- YAML configuration parsing and validation
    11→- Git operations (clone, branch, commit, push)
    12→- Diff validation and application utilities
    13→- GitLab MR integration with environment variable configuration
    14→- Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`) with full end-to-end workflow
    15→- Test mode infrastructure with mock implementations for CI/testing
    16→
    17→⚠️ **Partially Implemented:**
    18→- LangGraph healing (planner/reducer infrastructure exists, orchestration in progress)
    19→- Self-healing config parsing and data structures
    20→- Job templates for healing branches (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)
    21→
    22→❌ **Not Implemented:**
    23→- Model registry in `ployman` CLI
    24→- KB (knowledge base) read/write for learning
    25→
    26→### In-Scope (Must Work)
    27→
    28→- OpenRewrite recipe execution
    29→  - Reuse existing ARF/OpenRewrite pipeline (services/openrewrite-jvm, API/CLI `ploy arf transform`).
    30→  - Input: repo URL + branch, ordered recipe IDs. Output: local patch/commit applied to a workflow branch.
    31→
    32→ - LLM plan and exec
    33→  - `llm-exec` (Stream 2/Phase 1): run model with prompts + prefetched context (repo paths, HTTPS URLs), apply generated patch, commit; execute as Nomad jobs via internal/orchestration.
    34→  - `llm-plan` (Stream 2/Phase 2): produce a list of `llm-exec` steps (sequential only in MVP) from repo state + last error.
    35→  - Model registry: minimal CRUD in `ployman` CLI with schema validation; stored under `llms` namespace.
    36→  - MCP tools: declare per-step (MCP spec), env-only config; prefetch context per execution (no cache/persist).
    37→
    38→- Build check (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    39→  - Use existing build API to verify the code builds without deploying.
    40→  - Client: `internal/cli/common/deploy.go::SharedPush` (POST tarball to controller `POST /v1/apps/:app/builds?sha=...&env=dev[&lane=...]`).
    41→  - Server handler: `internal/build/trigger.go` performs lane detection (if lane not provided), builds artifacts (Unikraft/OSv/OCI/etc.), signs, generates SBOM, uploads to storage; it does not deploy to Nomad.
    42→  - Success criteria: HTTP 200; response includes `lane`, `image` or `dockerImage`. For logs, optional `GET /v1/apps/:app/logs`.
    43→  - Status endpoint (optional): `GET /v1/apps/:app/status` for additional visibility.
    44→  - App naming: derive unique app name `tfw-<transflow-id>-<timestamp>` unless explicitly provided.
    45→  - Lane override: allow explicit `lane` in transflow; fallback to auto-detect when absent.
    46→  - Timeout: request timeout configurable per run; default 10m for build check.
    47→
    48→ - Git operations + MR (Stream 3)
    49→  - Create a workflow branch at start; commit changes from steps; push branch to origin using provided token envs.
    50→  - GitLab MR: infer project from `target_repo`, target branch is `base_ref`; envs `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN`.
    51→  - Apply default MR labels/scope: `ploy`, `tfl` (if available).
    52→
    53→- LangGraph healing (planner/reducer as jobs)
    54→  - Planner job: Input = repo metadata + last_error + KB snapshot; Output = `plan.json` with parallel options: human‑step, llm‑exec, orw‑generated.
    55→  - Orchestrator fan‑out: run each option as an independent Nomad job (branch); first success wins; cancel others.
    56→  - Reducer job: Input = branch results + winner; Output = next actions (usually stop; else new `plan.json`).
    57→  - KB: cases and summaries persisted in SeaweedFS; locks via Consul KV; error/patch dedup via normalized signatures/fingerprints.
    58→  - Consistent learning & dedup: see `roadmap/transflow/kb.md`. Planner reads `summary.json`; branches write `cases/` and patch blobs; compactor maintains summaries and optional vector bundles.
    59→  - Job types:
    60→    - human-step: wait for human MR/commit; runner polls branch; success if build passes afterward.
    61→    - llm-exec: generate diff-only patch (MCP tools allowed); apply and build-check.
    62→    - orw-gen → openrewrite: generate ORW recipe (class/coords) via LLM; run OpenRewrite job; build-check.
    63→
    64→- Transflow runner (orchestrator)
    65→  - Parse `roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` and run steps sequentially: recipe → llm-plan → llm-exec → MR.
    66→  - Minimal logging; write per-step logs and final summary.
    67→
    68→### What Already Exists (Leverage)
    69→
    70→- OpenRewrite execution: `services/openrewrite-jvm`, ARF endpoints and CLI (`internal/cli/arf/*`, `api/arf/*`).
    71→- Git clone/diff/commit helpers: `api/arf/git_operations.go` (extend with push).
    72→- Lane/build/deploy system (not used in MVP flow): lane detection, Nomad templates, push deploy (`internal/cli/common/deploy.go`).
    73→- Git provider envs expected for MR (GitLab): set via environment variables.
    74→
    75→### New Work (Minimal)
    76→
    77→- New `transflow` orchestrator
    78→  - CLI entry: `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`.
    79→  - Step engine: call existing ARF recipe execution; implement LLM plan/exec runners; manage workflow branch lifecycle; run Build check before MR.
    80→
    81→- LLM runners
    82→  - `llm-exec`: container/job that receives model+prompts+context; produces unified diff or patch; apply and commit (used in parallel healing options).
    83→  - MCP injection via env; context prefetcher for repo files and HTTPS URLs.
    84→
    85→- Model registry
    86→  - `ployman models {list|get|add|update|delete}` with basic schema validation; stored alongside existing recipe metadata.
    87→
    88→ - MR integration (GitLab)
    89→  - Push branch using token env; call GitLab REST to create/update MR (project inferred from `target_repo` URL, source branch, target=`base_ref`), adding default labels `ploy`,`tfl` when supported.
    90→
    91→- Build check integration
    92→  - Implement a `build` step that tars the workspace and invokes `SharedPush` with `IsPlatform=false`, `Environment=dev`, and optional lane override from transflow.
    93→  - Expand `DeployConfig` to include `Timeout time.Duration`; `SharedPush` honors `config.Timeout` sourced from transflow's global `build_timeout`.
    94→  - Treat non-200 as build failure; record response JSON in step logs for diagnostics.
    95→
    96→ - LangGraph planner/reducer integration
    97→  - Add job templates and CLI/orchestrator glue to invoke planner after a failing build, and reducer after branch completion.
    98→  - Persist `plan.json`, branch job results, and a compact run manifest; first‑success‑wins cancellation.
    99→
   100→### Interfaces (High-Level)
   101→
   102→- Input: `roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` (id, repo, branch, steps; global lane/build_timeout).
   103→- Output: GitLab MR link, `plan.json`/branch results, step logs, final summary JSON.
   104→- Env: `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN` for MR; model/MCP creds via env only.
   105→
   106→### Out of Scope (MVP)
   107→
   108→- Parallel execution and branch-per-exec merging.
   109→- Secure network policy and allowlists.
   110→- Budgets/limits (tokens, cost, time).
   111→- Static analysis, vulnerability scans, compile gates.
   112→- Deploy/test gates and lane-driven runtime checks.
   113→- SeaweedFS/Consul KV persistence beyond what ARF already uses implicitly.
   114→- Auto-formatters outside of step-specific behavior.
   115→- End-to-end integration tests; coverage thresholds.
   116→
   117→### Risks and Mitigations
   118→
   119→- LLM determinism: constrain outputs to unified diff/patch; validate before apply.
   120→- Context quality: ensure prefetch resolves globs/URLs deterministically at run time; log sources.
   121→- MR creation: handle idempotency (update existing MR for same branch).
   122→
   123→### Next Steps
   124→
   125→1) Add `ploy transflow run` CLI + YAML parser. 2) Wire recipe step to ARF. 3) Implement LangGraph planner/reducer jobs and orchestrator fan‑out logic. 4) Implement LLM‑exec runner with MCP/env/context prefetch (for branch options). 5) Implement git push + GitLab MR creation. 6) Minimal logs and summary output.
   126→### Test Case: JDK 11 → 17 Migration
   127→
   128→- Minimal transform request (single OpenRewrite recipe step):
   129→
   130→POST /v1/transforms
   131→Content-Type: application/json
   132→
   133→{
   134→  "id": "java11to17",
   135→  "target_repo": "https://git.example.com/org/app.git",
   136→  "base_ref": "refs/heads/main",
   137→  "target_branch": "refs/heads/main",
   138→  "lane": "C",
   139→  "steps": [
   140→    {
   141→      "type": "recipe",
   142→      "engine": "openrewrite",
   143→      "recipes": [
   144→        "org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17"
   145→      ]
   146→    }
   147→  ]
   148→}
   149→
   150→- Expected flow:
   151→  1) Apply ORW recipe; commit on workflow/<id>/<timestamp>.
   152→  2) Build check via /v1/apps/:app/builds (no deploy).
   153→  3) If build fails, run LangGraph planner job; branches: human-step, llm-exec, orw-gen→openrewrite.
   154→  4) First success wins; reducer finalizes next actions (usually stop).
   155→
   156→## Test Repository
   157→
   158→**Java 11 Maven Test Repository for OpenRewrite Migration Testing:**
   159→- **URL**: https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-orw-java11-maven.git
   160→- **Purpose**: Real Java 11 codebase with OpenRewrite-transformable patterns for testing complete transflow workflows
   161→- **Contains**: Maven project structure, Java 11 code patterns (string operations, Optional usage, stream processing), JUnit 5 tests
   162→- **Used by**: Self-healing tests, integration tests, CLI validation with real repository scenarios
   163→- **Configuration**: Pre-configured with OpenRewrite Maven plugin for Java 11→17 migration recipes
   164→
   165→## CLI Usage and Examples
   166→
   167→### Basic Usage
   168→
   169→```bash
   170→# Run a complete transflow workflow
   171→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml
   172→
   173→# Run with verbose output
   174→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --verbose
   175→
   176→# Dry run to validate configuration
   177→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --dry-run
   178→
   179→# Use test mode for development/CI
   180→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --test-mode
   181→```
   182→
   183→### Configuration Examples
   184→
   185→**Basic Java 11→17 Migration:**
   186→```yaml
   187→# transflow.yaml
   188→version: v1alpha1
   189→id: java11-to-17-migration
   190→target_repo: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project.git
   191→target_branch: refs/heads/main
   192→base_ref: refs/heads/main
   193→lane: C
   194→build_timeout: 15m
   195→
   196→steps:
   197→  - type: recipe
   198→    id: java-migration
   199→    engine: openrewrite
   200→    recipes:
   201→      - org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17
   202→      - org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.CommonStaticAnalysis
   203→      - org.openrewrite.java.RemoveUnusedImports
   204→
   205→self_heal:
   206→  enabled: true
   207→  max_retries: 2
   208→  cooldown: 30s
   209→```
   210→
   211→**With GitLab MR Creation:**
   212→```bash
   213→# Set GitLab environment variables
   214→export GITLAB_URL=https://gitlab.com
   215→export GITLAB_TOKEN=your-gitlab-token
   216→
   217→# Run workflow with MR creation
   218→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml
   219→```
   220→
   221→**Multiple Recipe Steps:**
   222→```yaml
   223→version: v1alpha1
   224→id: comprehensive-cleanup
   225→target_repo: https://gitlab.com/your-org/legacy-project.git
   226→target_branch: refs/heads/modernization
   227→base_ref: refs/heads/main
   228→
   229→steps:
   230→  - type: recipe
   231→    id: import-cleanup
   232→    engine: openrewrite
   233→    recipes:
   234→      - org.openrewrite.java.RemoveUnusedImports
   235→      - org.openrewrite.java.OrderImports
   236→
   237→  - type: recipe
   238→    id: code-modernization
   239→    engine: openrewrite
   240→    recipes:
   241→      - org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.SimplifyBooleanExpression
   242→      - org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses
   243→      - org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17
   244→
   245→self_heal:
   246→  enabled: true
   247→  max_retries: 3
   248→```
   249→
   250→### Advanced Options
   251→
   252→**Specialized Execution Modes:**
   253→```bash
   254→# Execute only planner step (for debugging healing workflows)
   255→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --render-planner
   256→
   257→# Execute only LLM step with custom model
   258→TRANSFLOW_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06 \
   259→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --exec-llm-first
   260→
   261→# Execute OpenRewrite application step
   262→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --exec-orw-first
   263→
   264→# Apply first successful transformation and stop
   265→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --apply-first
   266→```
   267→
   268→**Testing and Development:**
   269→```bash
   270→# Test mode - uses mock implementations for all external services
   271→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --test-mode
   272→
   273→# Plan mode - shows execution plan without running
   274→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --plan
   275→
   276→# Reduce mode - processes healing results
   277→ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --reduce
   278→```
   279→
   280→### Expected Workflow Output
   281→
   282→```
   283→Starting Transflow: java11-to-17-migration
   284→✓ Cloning repository: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project.git
   285→✓ Creating branch: workflow/java11-to-17-migration/20250905151234
   286→✓ Executing recipe: org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17
   287→✓ Committing changes: Apply Java 11 to 17 migration recipes
   288→✓ Building project: tfw-java11-to-17-migration-20250905151234
   289→✓ Pushing branch to remote
   290→✓ Creating GitLab MR: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project/-/merge_requests/42
   291→
   292→Workflow completed successfully!
   293→  Branch: workflow/java11-to-17-migration/20250905151234
   294→  Build Version: 20250905151234-abc123
   295→  Duration: 2m 34s
   296→  MR URL: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project/-/merge_requests/42
   297→```
   298→
   299→### Error Handling and Self-Healing
   300→
   301→When builds fail and self-healing is enabled, the system will:
   302→
   303→1. **Analyze the failure** using LangGraph planner
   304→2. **Generate healing options** (human intervention, LLM fixes, additional recipes)
   305→3. **Execute options in parallel** with first-success-wins logic
   306→4. **Apply successful fix** and continue workflow
   307→5. **Create MR** with healing summary included
   308→
   309→**Self-Healing Output Example:**
   310→```
   311→⚠ Build failed: compilation errors in Main.java:15
   312→🔧 Self-healing enabled, starting recovery...
   313→✓ Planner job completed: 3 healing options generated
   314→  → Option 1: llm-exec (confidence: 0.8)
   315→  → Option 2: org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses
   316→  → Option 3: human-step
   317→✓ Executing healing options in parallel...
   318→✓ llm-exec option succeeded after 45s
   319→✓ Retrying build with healed changes...
   320→✓ Build successful: tfw-java11-to-17-migration-20250905151234-healed
   321→✓ Continuing workflow...
   322→```
   323→
   324→### Environment Configuration
   325→
   326→**Required Environment Variables:**
   327→- `GITLAB_TOKEN`: GitLab personal access token for MR creation
   328→- `GITLAB_URL`: GitLab instance URL (default: https://gitlab.com)
   329→
   330→**Optional Environment Variables:**
   331→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL`: LLM model for healing (default: gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06)
   332→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS`: MCP tools configuration JSON
   333→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS`: Execution limits configuration JSON
   334→- `NOMAD_ADDR`: Nomad cluster address for job submission
   335→
   336→### Integration with Existing Systems
   337→
   338→The transflow CLI integrates seamlessly with existing Ploy infrastructure:
   339→- **ARF Pipeline**: Reuses `ploy arf transform` for recipe execution
   340→- **Build System**: Uses existing `/v1/apps/:app/builds` API for validation
   341→- **Git Operations**: Leverages ARF git handling with automatic configuration
   342→- **Job Orchestration**: Uses existing Nomad job submission infrastructure
   343→

1→# Transflow CLI Module CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→Complete CLI integration for orchestrating multi-step code transformation workflows with automated build validation, self-healing capabilities, and GitLab merge request integration.
     5→
     6→## Narrative Summary
     7→The transflow module provides end-to-end implementation of `ploy transflow run` command supporting complete transformation pipelines. It applies code transformations via OpenRewrite recipes, validates results through automated builds, creates GitLab merge requests for review, and includes sophisticated self-healing workflows with LangGraph-based job orchestration.
     8→
     9→Core workflow: clone repository → create branch → apply transformations → commit changes → validate build → create/update merge request → optional healing on failures. The module integrates with ARF infrastructure for recipe execution, SharedPush for build validation, and includes comprehensive job submission infrastructure for healing workflows.
    10→
    11→## Key Files
    12→- `run.go:1-250` - CLI command entry point and flag parsing
    13→- `runner.go:1-650` - Complete orchestration logic with healing integration
    14→- `runner.go:126-172` - Main dependency injection and initialization
    15→- `runner.go:174-280` - Core workflow execution (Run method)
    16→- `runner.go:282-380` - Build validation and error handling
    17→- `runner.go:441-500` - Self-healing workflow integration
    18→- `runner.go:509-553` - GitLab MR creation and updates
    19→- `config.go:1-150` - Configuration loading, validation, and timeout parsing
    20→- `integrations.go:87-200` - Factory pattern for production vs test implementations
    21→- `types.go:1-120` - Job submission type system for healing workflows
    22→- `job_submission.go:1-120` - JobSubmissionHelper implementation
    23→- `fanout_orchestrator.go:1-150` - Parallel healing branch orchestration
    24→- `self_healing.go:1-250` - Self-healing configuration and result tracking
    25→- `mocks.go:1-200` - Complete mock implementation framework
    26→- `integration_test.go:1-300` - End-to-end integration test suite
    27→
    28→## Integration Points
    29→### Consumes
    30→- ARF Git Operations: Repository cloning, branching, commits
    31→- ARF Recipe Executor: OpenRewrite recipe execution
    32→- SharedPush Build Checker: Build validation and deployment
    33→- GitLab REST API: Merge request creation/updates (via provider.GitProvider)
    34→- Ploy Orchestration: Job submission for healing workflows
    35→
    36→### Provides
    37→- CLI Commands: `ploy transflow run -f <config>` with complete flag support
    38→- Workflow Execution: End-to-end transformation pipeline with result tracking
    39→- Self-Healing: LangGraph-based healing with planner/reducer jobs and parallel branch execution
    40→- MR Integration: GitLab merge request creation/updates with rich descriptions
    41→- Test Mode: Complete mock infrastructure for CI/CD and local testing
    42→- Job Orchestration: Nomad job submission and monitoring for healing workflows
    43→
    44→## Configuration
    45→Required files:
    46→- `transflow.yaml` - Workflow configuration with steps and target repository
    47→
    48→Environment variables:
    49→- `GITLAB_URL` - GitLab instance URL (defaults to https://gitlab.com)
    50→- `GITLAB_TOKEN` - GitLab API token for MR operations
    51→- `TRANSFLOW_SUBMIT` - Set to "1" to enable job submission for healing workflows
    52→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL` - LLM model for healing (default: gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06)
    53→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS` - Tool configuration JSON for healing jobs
    54→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS` - Execution limits JSON for healing jobs
    55→- `NOMAD_ADDR` - Nomad cluster address for job submission
    56→
    57→CLI flags:
    58→- `--config, -f` - Configuration file path
    59→- `--test-mode` - Enable mock implementations for testing
    60→- `--dry-run` - Show what would be done without execution
    61→- `--verbose` - Detailed output during workflow execution
    62→
    63→## Key Patterns
    64→- Complete dependency injection with interface-based design (see runner.go:126-172, integrations.go:87-200)
    65→- Factory pattern for production vs test implementations (see integrations.go)
    66→- Test mode infrastructure with comprehensive mocking (see mocks.go:1-200)
    67→- Job submission infrastructure with type-safe interfaces (see types.go, job_submission.go)
    68→- Fanout orchestration with first-success-wins semantics (see fanout_orchestrator.go)
    69→- Graceful error handling with optional MR creation (see runner.go:509-553)
    70→- Self-healing workflow with LangGraph integration and parallel branch execution
    71→- Configuration validation with timeout parsing and comprehensive error reporting
    72→
    73→## Related Documentation
    74→- `../git/provider/CLAUDE.md` - GitLab provider implementation
    75→- `../../api/arf/` - Application Recipe Framework integration
    76→- `../../orchestration/` - Job submission and monitoring infrastructure
    77→- `../../../roadmap/transflow/MVP.md` - Complete implementation status and requirements
    78→- Integration test repository: https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-orw-java11-maven.git

content.text: Now let me examine the existing transflow implementation to understand the current architecture:

content.content:
1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"path/filepath"
     8→	"strings"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/cli/common"
    12→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/git/provider"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// GitOperationsInterface defines the Git operations needed by the runner
    16→type GitOperationsInterface interface {
    17→	CloneRepository(ctx context.Context, repoURL, branch, targetPath string) error
    18→	CreateBranchAndCheckout(ctx context.Context, repoPath, branchName string) error
    19→	CommitChanges(ctx context.Context, repoPath, message string) error
    20→	PushBranch(ctx context.Context, repoPath, remoteURL, branchName string) error
    21→}
    22→
    23→// RecipeExecutorInterface defines the recipe execution interface
    24→type RecipeExecutorInterface interface {
    25→	ExecuteRecipes(ctx context.Context, workspacePath string, recipeIDs []string) error
    26→}
    27→
    28→// BuildCheckerInterface defines the build check interface
    29→type BuildCheckerInterface interface {
    30→	CheckBuild(ctx context.Context, config common.DeployConfig) (*common.DeployResult, error)
    31→}
    32→
    33→// StepResult represents the result of executing a single step
    34→type StepResult struct {
    35→	StepID   string
    36→	Success  bool
    37→	Message  string
    38→	Duration time.Duration
    39→}
    40→
    41→// TransflowResult represents the overall result of a transflow execution
    42→type TransflowResult struct {
    43→	Success        bool
    44→	WorkflowID     string
    45→	BranchName     string
    46→	CommitSHA      string
    47→	BuildVersion   string
    48→	StepResults    []StepResult
    49→	ErrorMessage   string
    50→	Duration       time.Duration
    51→	HealingSummary *TransflowHealingSummary
    52→	MRURL          string // GitLab merge request URL if created
    53→}
    54→
    55→// Summary returns a human-readable summary of the transflow execution
    56→func (r *TransflowResult) Summary() string {
    57→	var sb strings.Builder
    58→
    59→	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Workflow: %s\n", r.WorkflowID))
    60→
    61→	if r.Success {
    62→		sb.WriteString("Status: SUCCESS\n")
    63→	} else {
    64→		sb.WriteString("Status: FAILED\n")
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	if r.BranchName != "" {
    68→		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Branch: %s\n", r.BranchName))
    69→	}
    70→
    71→	if r.CommitSHA != "" {
    72→		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Commit: %s\n", r.CommitSHA))
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	if r.BuildVersion != "" {
    76→		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Build: %s\n", r.BuildVersion))
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	if !r.Success && r.ErrorMessage != "" {
    80→		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Error: %s\n", r.ErrorMessage))
    81→	}
    82→
    83→	sb.WriteString("Steps:\n")
    84→	for _, step := range r.StepResults {
    85→		status := "✓"
    86→		if !step.Success {
    87→			status = "✗"
    88→		}
    89→		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  %s %s: %s\n", status, step.StepID, step.Message))
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	// Include healing summary if self-healing was enabled
    93→	if r.HealingSummary != nil && r.HealingSummary.Enabled {
    94→		sb.WriteString("\nSelf-Healing:\n")
    95→		if r.HealingSummary.AttemptsCount > 0 {
    96→			sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  Attempts: %d/%d\n", r.HealingSummary.AttemptsCount, r.HealingSummary.MaxRetries))
    97→			sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  Successful fixes: %d\n", r.HealingSummary.TotalHealed))
    98→			sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  Final result: %s\n", map[bool]string{true: "SUCCESS", false: "FAILED"}[r.HealingSummary.FinalSuccess]))
    99→
   100→			for _, attempt := range r.HealingSummary.Attempts {

1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→// Job submission types for transflow healing workflow
     9→
    10→// JobSpec describes a job to be submitted to the orchestrator
    11→type JobSpec struct {
    12→	Name    string                 `json:"name"`
    13→	Type    string                 `json:"type"`
    14→	HCLPath string                 `json:"hcl_path"`
    15→	EnvVars map[string]string      `json:"env_vars"`
    16→	Timeout time.Duration          `json:"timeout"`
    17→	Inputs  map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
    18→}
    19→
    20→// JobResult contains the result of a completed job
    21→type JobResult struct {
    22→	JobID     string            `json:"job_id"`
    23→	Status    string            `json:"status"` // "completed", "failed", "timeout"
    24→	Output    string            `json:"output"`
    25→	Error     string            `json:"error"`
    26→	Duration  time.Duration     `json:"duration"`
    27→	Artifacts map[string]string `json:"artifacts"`
    28→}
    29→
    30→// PlanResult contains the output from a planner job
    31→type PlanResult struct {
    32→	PlanID  string                   `json:"plan_id"`
    33→	Options []map[string]interface{} `json:"options"`
    34→}
    35→
    36→// NextAction describes what the reducer determined should happen next
    37→type NextAction struct {
    38→	Action string `json:"action"`
    39→	Notes  string `json:"notes"`
    40→}
    41→
    42→// BranchSpec describes a healing branch to execute
    43→type BranchSpec struct {
    44→	ID     string                 `json:"id"`
    45→	Type   string                 `json:"type"`
    46→	Inputs map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// BranchResult contains the result of executing a healing branch
    50→type BranchResult struct {
    51→	ID         string        `json:"id"`
    52→	Status     string        `json:"status"`
    53→	JobID      string        `json:"job_id"`
    54→	Notes      string        `json:"notes"`
    55→	StartedAt  time.Time     `json:"started_at"`
    56→	FinishedAt time.Time     `json:"finished_at"`
    57→	Duration   time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    58→}
    59→
    60→// Interface definitions
    61→
    62→// JobSubmissionHelper provides methods for submitting transflow jobs
    63→type JobSubmissionHelper interface {
    64→	SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, config *TransflowConfig, buildError string, workspace string) (*PlanResult, error)
    65→	SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, planID string, results []BranchResult, winner *BranchResult, workspace string) (*NextAction, error)
    66→}
    67→
    68→// FanoutOrchestrator manages parallel execution of healing branches
    69→type FanoutOrchestrator interface {
    70→	RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error)
    71→}
    72→

1→---
     2→task: [prefix]-[descriptive-name]
     3→branch: feature/[name]|fix/[name]|experiment/[name]|none
     4→status: pending|in-progress|completed|blocked
     5→created: YYYY-MM-DD
     6→modules: [list of services/modules involved]
     7→---
     8→
     9→# [Human-Readable Title]
    10→
    11→## Problem/Goal
    12→[Clear description of what we're solving/building]
    13→
    14→## Success Criteria
    15→- [ ] Specific, measurable outcome
    16→- [ ] Another concrete goal
    17→
    18→## Context Files
    19→<!-- Added by context-gathering agent or manually -->
    20→- @service/file.py:123-145  # Specific lines
    21→- @other/module.py          # Whole file  
    22→- patterns/auth-flow        # Pattern reference
    23→

    25→<!-- Any specific notes or requirements from the developer -->
    26→
    27→## Work Log
    28→<!-- Updated as work progresses -->
